papers.title: The Photo-Taking Impairment Effect in Educational Contexts
papers.abstract: This seminal study demonstrates that taking photographs of objects impairs memory for those objects, with implications for classroom photo-taking behavior.

papers.title: Teacher Attitudes Toward Student Use of Mobile Devices in Class
papers.abstract: A comprehensive study of teacher attitudes toward student use of mobile devices, including photography, in the classroom.

papers.title: Dial D for Distraction: The Making and Breaking of Cell Phone Policies in the College Classroom
papers.abstract: This study examines the development and implementation of cell phone policies in college classrooms.

papers.title: Tensions between technology integration practices of teachers and ICT in education policy expectations
papers.abstract: In line with ICT in education policies and curricula in Ghana and many other countries, teachers are expected to integrate digital technologies into instruction.

papers.title: Digital Photography in Education: A Review of Literature
papers.abstract: A comprehensive review of literature on the use of digital photography in educational settings, examining its impact on learning outcomes.

papers.title: Multitasking With Smartphones in the College Classroom
papers.abstract: This study examines multitasking behaviors with smartphones in college classrooms.

papers.title: Smartphone Apps in Education: Students Create Videos to Teach Smartphone Use as Tool for Learning
papers.abstract: Smartphones are regular classroom accessories. Educators should work with children to understand the capacity of smartphones for learning and civic engagement.

papers.title: Photo-Taking Behavior in Educational Settings: Cognitive and Social Motivations
papers.abstract: An investigation into the cognitive and social motivations that drive students to take photographs during class sessions.

papers.title: Understanding Student Photo-Taking: A Mixed-Methods Approach
papers.abstract: A mixed-methods study examining the reasons why students take photos during lectures and how this behavior affects their learning outcomes.

papers.title: Smartphones at School: A Mixed-Methods Analysis of Educators' and Students' Perspectives on Mobile Phone Use at School
papers.abstract: As smartphone ownership and use by children and youth has increased over the past decade, so has the presence of phones within the classroom.
